57	def _zero_padded_pcg_tail(buf: torch.Tensor, context) -> None:
58	    """Zero the padded tail ``buf`` leaves as torch.empty garbage under PCG
59	    replay, so NaN/Inf cannot reach residual / MoE routing / allreduce."""
60	    pcg_static_tokens = context.num_tokens
61	    actual_tokens = context.raw_num_tokens
62	    if (
63	        pcg_static_tokens is not None
64	        and actual_tokens is not None
65	        and pcg_static_tokens > actual_tokens
66	    ):
67	        first_dim = buf.shape[0]
68	        elems_per_token = buf.numel() // first_dim
69	        buf.view(first_dim, elems_per_token)[actual_tokens:].zero_()
70	
71	
72	if TYPE_CHECKING:
73	    from sglang.srt.layers.quantization.base_config import QuantizationConfig
74	    from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_batch_info import ForwardBatch
75	
76	
77	class AttentionType(Enum):
78	    """
79	    Attention type.
80	    Use string to be compatible with `torch.compile`.
81	    """
82	
83	    # Decoder attention between previous layer Q/K/V
84	    DECODER = "decoder"
85	    # Decoder bidirectional attention between image tokens
86	    DECODER_BIDIRECTIONAL = "decoder_bidirectional"
87	    # Encoder attention between previous layer Q/K/V
88	    ENCODER_ONLY = "encoder_only"
89	
90	
91	class RadixAttention(nn.Module):
92	    """
93	    The attention layer implementation.
94	    """
95	
96	    def __init__(
97	        self,
98	        num_heads: int,
99	        head_dim: int,
100	        scaling: float,
101	        num_kv_heads: int,
102	        layer_id: int,
103	        logit_cap: float = 0.0,
104	        v_head_dim: int = -1,
105	        sliding_window_size: int = -1,
106	        is_cross_attention: bool = False,
107	        pos_encoding_mode: str = "NONE",
108	        logit_capping_method: str = "tanh",
109	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
110	        attn_type: AttentionType = AttentionType.DECODER,
111	        use_irope: bool = False,
112	        prefix: str = "",
113	    ):
114	        super().__init__()
115	        self.tp_q_head_num = num_heads
116	        self.tp_k_head_num = num_kv_heads
117	        self.tp_v_head_num = num_kv_heads
118	        self.head_dim = head_dim
119	        self.qk_head_dim = head_dim
120	        self.v_head_dim = v_head_dim if v_head_dim != -1 else head_dim
121	        self.scaling = scaling
122	        self.layer_id = layer_id
123	        self.logit_cap = logit_cap
124	        self.sliding_window_size = sliding_window_size or -1
125	        self.is_cross_attention = is_cross_attention
126	        self.use_irope = use_irope
127	        self.k_scale = None
128	        self.v_scale = None
129	        self.k_scale_float = None
130	        self.v_scale_float = None
131	        self.quant_method = None
132	
133	        if quant_config is not None:
134	            self.quant_method = quant_config.get_quant_method(self, prefix=prefix)
135	        if self.quant_method is not None:
136	            self.quant_method.create_weights(self)
137	        self.attn_type = attn_type
138	
139	        self.pos_encoding_mode = pos_encoding_mode
140	        self.logit_capping_method = logit_capping_method
141	        self.xai_temperature_len = -1
142	
143	    def forward(
144	        self,
145	        q,
146	        k,
147	        v,
148	        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
149	        save_kv_cache: bool = True,
150	        key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
151	        **kwargs,
152	    ):
153	        if k is not None:
154	            # For cross-layer sharing, kv can be None
155	            assert v is not None
156	            if "k_rope" not in kwargs:
157	                k = k.view(-1, self.tp_k_head_num, self.qk_head_dim)
158	                v = v.view(-1, self.tp_v_head_num, self.v_head_dim)
159	            else:
160	                k = k.view(-1, self.tp_k_head_num, self.v_head_dim)
161	
162	        context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
163	        if (
164	            forward_batch.forward_mode.is_extend()
165	            and context is not None
166	            # ``_force_eager_attn`` is only set inside Inkling's eager
167	            # norm+attn+sconv region, never during tc-piecewise capture. Reading
168	            # the ContextVar under the fullgraph torch.compile trace is
169	            # untraceable ("Unsupported method call: ContextVar.get"), so
170	            # short-circuit it while compiling -- force-eager is always off there.
171	            and (torch.compiler.is_compiling() or not _force_eager_attn.get())
172	        ):
173	            if kwargs.get("idx_q") is not None:
174	                if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
175	                    return get_attn_backend().forward(
176	                        q, k, v, self, forward_batch, save_kv_cache, **kwargs
177	                    )
178	                idx_q = kwargs["idx_q"]
179	                idx_k = kwargs["idx_k"]
180	                idx_v = kwargs.get("idx_v")
181	                attn_out = q.new_empty(
182	                    (q.shape[0], self.tp_q_head_num * self.v_head_dim)
183	                )
184	                idx_out = q.new_empty((q.shape[0], idx_q.shape[1] * idx_q.shape[2]))
185	                unified_sparse_attention_with_output(
186	                    q,
187	                    k,
188	                    v,
189	                    attn_out,
190	                    idx_out,
191	                    idx_q,
192	                    idx_k,
193	                    save_kv_cache,
194	                    self.layer_id,
195	                    idx_v=idx_v,
196	                )
197	                return idx_out, attn_out
198	            # FP8 q (e.g. mxfp8 KV-cache attention) still produces a bf16
199	            # attention output; sizing the buffer off q's dtype would silently
200	            # cast-copy the result to fp8.
201	            out_dtype = (
202	                torch.bfloat16
203	                if q.dtype in (torch.float8_e4m3fn, torch.float8_e5m2)
204	                else q.dtype
205	            )
206	            if self.qk_head_dim != self.v_head_dim:
207	                output = q.new_empty(
208	                    (q.shape[0], self.tp_q_head_num * self.v_head_dim),
209	                    dtype=out_dtype,
210	                )
211	            else:
212	                output = torch.empty_like(q, dtype=out_dtype)
213	            if any(
214	                key in kwargs
215	                for key in (
216	                    "score_mod",
217	                    "aux_tensors",
218	                    "rel_bias",
219	                    "q_descale",
220	                    "k_descale",
221	                    "v_descale",
222	                )
223	            ):
224	                # A score_mod callable, aux_tensors, rel_bias, or mxfp8 descale
225	                # tensors can't cross the unified_attention_with_output custom-op
226	                # schema; route this backend's extend attention through the plain
227	                # eager path.
228	                if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
229	                    breakable_attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
230	                        q, k, v, output, save_kv_cache, self.layer_id, kwargs
231	                    )
232	                else:
233	                    attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
234	                        q, k, v, output, save_kv_cache, self.layer_id, kwargs
235	                    )
236	                return output
237	            # Chunked-prefix MHA needs LSE to merge independently normalized
238	            # suffix and cached-prefix attention states.
239	            return_lse = bool(forward_batch.mha_return_lse)
240	            mha_companion_layers = context.mha_companion_layers
241	            use_mha_companion = (
242	                mha_companion_layers is not None
243	                and mha_companion_layers[self.layer_id] is self
244	            )
245	            if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
246	                op = (
247	                    breakable_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
248	                    if return_lse
249	                    else breakable_unified_attention_with_output
250	                )
251	            else:
252	                op = (
253	                    unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
254	                    if return_lse
255	                    else unified_attention_with_output
256	                )
257	            lse = op(
258	                q,
259	                k,
260	                v,
261	                output,
262	                save_kv_cache,
263	                self.layer_id,
264	                use_mha_companion=use_mha_companion,
265	                key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
266	                **kwargs,
267	            )
268	            if return_lse:
269	                return output.view(-1, self.tp_q_head_num, self.v_head_dim), lse
270	            return output
271	        else:
272	            return get_attn_backend().forward(
273	                q,
274	                k,
275	                v,
276	                self,
277	                forward_batch,
278	                save_kv_cache,
279	                **kwargs,
280	            )
281	
282	
283	def _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
284	    query: torch.Tensor,
285	    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
286	    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
287	    output: torch.Tensor,
288	    save_kv_cache: bool,
289	    layer_id: int,
290	    use_mha_companion: bool,
291	    return_lse: bool,
292	    *,
293	    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
294	    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
295	    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
296	    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
297	    # MLA / TRT-LLM / NSA paths pass these through RadixAttention.forward(**kwargs);
298	    # they must appear in the schema when --enforce-piecewise-cuda-graph is on.
299	    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
300	    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
301	    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
302	    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
303	) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
304	    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
305	    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
306	    attention_layers = context.attention_layers
307	    attention_layer = attention_layers[layer_id]
308	    real_query_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu
309	    # Ordinary PCG attention pads Q/K/V to the same token bucket. Prefix MHA
310	    # instead supplies a fixed-capacity K/V chunk whose extent is independent
311	    # of the suffix queries, so its caller must preserve that separate extent.
312	    if key_value_num_tokens is None:
313	        key_value_num_tokens = real_query_num_tokens
314	
315	    query = query[:real_query_num_tokens]
316	    if key is not None:
317	        key = key[:key_value_num_tokens]
318	    if value is not None:
319	        value = value[:key_value_num_tokens]
320	
321	    # DeepSeek MLA has two RadixAttention instances per layer (attn_mqa and
322	    # attn_mha) that share the same layer_id. Preserve the calling instance's
323	    # identity through the custom-op boundary; save_kv_cache is not an identity
324	    # signal because absorbed MLA can also disable a redundant cache store.
325	    if use_mha_companion:
326	        assert context.mha_companion_layers is not None
327	        attention_layer = context.mha_companion_layers[layer_id]
328	        assert attention_layer is not None
329	
330	    kwargs = {}
331	    if q_rope is not None:
332	        kwargs["q_rope"] = q_rope[:real_query_num_tokens]
333	    if k_rope is not None:
334	        kwargs["k_rope"] = k_rope[:key_value_num_tokens]
335	    if sinks is not None:
336	        kwargs["sinks"] = sinks
337	    if cos_sin_cache is not None:
338	        kwargs["cos_sin_cache"] = cos_sin_cache
339	    if is_neox is not None:
340	        kwargs["is_neox"] = is_neox
341	    if llama_4_scaling is not None:
342	        kwargs["llama_4_scaling"] = llama_4_scaling
343	    if topk_indices is not None:
344	        kwargs["topk_indices"] = topk_indices[:real_query_num_tokens]
345	
346	    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
347	    # Keep the original ForwardBatch object and only narrow cache locations for
348	    # this backend call so model/backend state is still written to the same batch.
349	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_query_num_tokens]
350	
351	    # Store pre-allocated output for FA backend to write directly into.
352	    # Must slice to real_query_num_tokens to match the narrowed query shape —
353	    # the FA kernel validates out.size(0) == q.size(0).
354	    forward_batch._attn_output = output[:real_query_num_tokens]
355	
356	    ret = get_attn_backend().forward(
357	        query,
358	        key,
359	        value,
360	        attention_layer,
361	        forward_batch,
362	        save_kv_cache,
363	        **kwargs,
364	    )
365	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc
366	
367	    lse = None
368	    if return_lse:
369	        assert isinstance(ret, tuple)
370	        ret, lse, *_ = ret
371	    else:
372	        assert isinstance(ret, torch.Tensor)
373	
374	    if ret.data_ptr() != output.data_ptr():
375	        output[:real_query_num_tokens].view(ret.shape).copy_(ret)
376	
377	    # During PCG replay the attention backend writes only the narrowed
378	    # real-token slice (output[:real_query_num_tokens]) and leaves padded positions
379	    # as uninitialized torch.empty garbage. Zero them so garbage (NaN/Inf) does
380	    # not propagate through residual connections, MoE routing, and allreduce.
381	    # This affects every backend that varlen-writes under PCG, not just ROCm.
382	    # Use context.raw_num_tokens (pre-padding count from PCG runner) instead of
383	    # forward_batch.extend_num_tokens, which is None for TARGET_VERIFY batches.
384	    _zero_padded_pcg_tail(output, context)
385	    if lse is not None and lse.shape[0] != output.shape[0]:
386	        padded_lse = lse.new_zeros((output.shape[0], *lse.shape[1:]))
387	        padded_lse[:real_query_num_tokens].copy_(lse)
388	        lse = padded_lse
389	    return lse
390	
391	
392	@register_custom_op(mutates_args=["output"])
393	@register_split_op()
394	def unified_attention_with_output(
395	    query: torch.Tensor,
396	    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
397	    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
398	    output: torch.Tensor,
399	    save_kv_cache: bool,
400	    layer_id: int,
401	    *,
402	    use_mha_companion: bool = False,
403	    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
404	    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
405	    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
406	    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
407	    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
408	    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
409	    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
410	    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
411	) -> None:
412	    _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
413	        query,
414	        key,
415	        value,
416	        output,
417	        save_kv_cache,
418	        layer_id,
419	        use_mha_companion,
420	        False,
421	        key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
422	        q_rope=q_rope,
423	        k_rope=k_rope,
424	        sinks=sinks,
425	        cos_sin_cache=cos_sin_cache,
426	        is_neox=is_neox,
427	        llama_4_scaling=llama_4_scaling,
428	        topk_indices=topk_indices,
429	    )
430	
431	
432	def _unified_attention_with_output_and_lse_fake(
433	    query: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs
434	) -> torch.Tensor:
435	    return query.new_empty((query.shape[0], query.shape[1]), dtype=torch.float32)
436	
437	
438	@register_custom_op(
439	    mutates_args=["output"], fake_impl=_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse_fake
440	)
441	@register_split_op()
442	def unified_attention_with_output_and_lse(
443	    query: torch.Tensor,
444	    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
445	    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
446	    output: torch.Tensor,
447	    save_kv_cache: bool,
448	    layer_id: int,
449	    *,
450	    use_mha_companion: bool = False,
451	    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
452	    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
453	    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
454	    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
455	    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
456	    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
457	    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
458	    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
459	) -> torch.Tensor:
460	    lse = _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
461	        query,
462	        key,
463	        value,
464	        output,
465	        save_kv_cache,
466	        layer_id,
467	        use_mha_companion,
468	        True,
469	        key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
470	        q_rope=q_rope,
471	        k_rope=k_rope,
472	        sinks=sinks,
473	        cos_sin_cache=cos_sin_cache,
474	        is_neox=is_neox,
475	        llama_4_scaling=llama_4_scaling,
476	        topk_indices=topk_indices,
477	    )
478	    assert lse is not None
479	    return lse
480	
481	
482	@register_custom_op(mutates_args=["attn_out", "idx_out"])
483	@register_split_op()
484	def unified_sparse_attention_with_output(
485	    query: torch.Tensor,
486	    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
487	    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
488	    attn_out: torch.Tensor,
489	    idx_out: torch.Tensor,
490	    idx_q: torch.Tensor,
491	    idx_k: torch.Tensor,
492	    save_kv_cache: bool,
493	    layer_id: int,
494	    *,
495	    idx_v: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
496	) -> None:
497	    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
498	    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
499	    attention_layer = context.attention_layers[layer_id]
500	    real_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu
501	
502	    query = query[:real_num_tokens]
503	    if key is not None:
504	        key = key[:real_num_tokens]
505	    if value is not None:
506	        value = value[:real_num_tokens]
507	    idx_q = idx_q[:real_num_tokens]
508	    idx_k = idx_k[:real_num_tokens]
509	    if idx_v is not None:
510	        idx_v = idx_v[:real_num_tokens]
511	
512	    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
513	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_num_tokens]
514	
515	    ret_idx, ret_out = get_attn_backend().forward(
516	        query,
517	        key,
518	        value,
519	        attention_layer,
520	        forward_batch,
521	        save_kv_cache,
522	        idx_q=idx_q,
523	        idx_k=idx_k,
524	        idx_v=idx_v,
525	    )
526	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc
527	
528	    attn_out[:real_num_tokens].view(ret_out.shape).copy_(ret_out)
529	    # disable_value layers return ret_idx=None; the guard keeps idx_out's
530	    # untouched real-token slice safe (model returns before index_o_proj).
531	    if ret_idx is not None:
532	        idx_out[:real_num_tokens].view(ret_idx.shape).copy_(ret_idx)
533	
534	    for buf in (attn_out, idx_out):
535	        _zero_padded_pcg_tail(buf, context)
536	    return
537	
538	
539	breakable_unified_attention_with_output = eager_on_graph(True)(
540	    unified_attention_with_output
541	)
542	breakable_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse = eager_on_graph(True)(
543	    unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
544	)
545	
546	
547	def attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
548	    query: torch.Tensor,
549	    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
550	    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
551	    output: torch.Tensor,
552	    save_kv_cache: bool,
553	    layer_id: int,
554	    extra_kwargs: dict,
555	) -> None:
556	    """Breakable/tc_piecewise attention for backends whose forward needs kwargs
557	    that cannot cross the ``unified_attention_with_output`` custom-op schema --
558	    a ``score_mod`` callable and/or ``aux_tensors`` (e.g. Inkling's relative-bias
559	    fa4 attention). Plain (not a custom op) so the callable passes through; still
560	    runs eagerly between graph segments under BCG via the wrapper below. Mirrors
561	    the real-token narrowing + padded-output write of
562	    ``unified_attention_with_output``, and narrows per-token ``aux_tensors`` too.
563	    """
564	    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
565	    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
566	    attention_layer = context.attention_layers[layer_id]
567	    real_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu
568	
569	    query = query[:real_num_tokens]
570	    if key is not None:
571	        key = key[:real_num_tokens]
572	    if value is not None:
573	        value = value[:real_num_tokens]
574	
575	    kwargs = dict(extra_kwargs)
576	    aux_tensors = kwargs.get("aux_tensors")
577	    if aux_tensors is not None:
578	        kwargs["aux_tensors"] = [t[:real_num_tokens] for t in aux_tensors]
579	    for per_token_key in ("rel_bias", "q_descale", "k_descale", "v_descale"):
580	        t = kwargs.get(per_token_key)
581	        if t is not None:
582	            kwargs[per_token_key] = t[:real_num_tokens]
583	
584	    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
585	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_num_tokens]
586	    forward_batch._attn_output = output[:real_num_tokens]
587	
588	    ret = get_attn_backend().forward(
589	        query, key, value, attention_layer, forward_batch, save_kv_cache, **kwargs
590	    )
591	    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc
592	
593	    if ret.data_ptr() != output.data_ptr():
594	        output[:real_num_tokens].view(ret.shape).copy_(ret)
595	
596	    if _is_hip:
597	        _zero_padded_pcg_tail(output, context)
598	    return
599	
600	
601	breakable_attention_with_output_extra_kwargs = eager_on_graph(True)(
602	    attention_with_output_extra_kwargs
603	)
604

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Radix attention."""

from __future__ import annotations

from contextlib import contextmanager
from contextvars import ContextVar
from enum import Enum
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Optional

import torch
from torch import nn

from sglang.srt.compilation.compilation_config import register_split_op
from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_context import get_attn_backend
from sglang.srt.model_executor.runner_backend_utils.breakable_cuda_graph import (
    eager_on_graph,
    is_in_breakable_cuda_graph,
)
from sglang.srt.model_executor.runner_backend_utils.tc_piecewise_cuda_graph import (
    get_tc_piecewise_forward_context,
)
from sglang.srt.utils.common import is_hip
from sglang.srt.utils.custom_op import register_custom_op

_is_hip = is_hip()

# When set, RadixAttention.forward runs the attention backend eagerly instead of
# routing through the tc-piecewise split op. A caller already inside a
# breakable-CUDA-graph eager break (e.g. Inkling wrapping norm+attn+sconv in one eager
# region for multi-seq-correct short-conv metadata) sets this so the attn does not
# start a nested break (which would assert on the ended segment). Default off.
_force_eager_attn: ContextVar[bool] = ContextVar("_force_eager_attn", default=False)


@contextmanager
def force_eager_attention():
    token = _force_eager_attn.set(True)
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        _force_eager_attn.reset(token)


def _zero_padded_pcg_tail(buf: torch.Tensor, context) -> None:
    """Zero the padded tail ``buf`` leaves as torch.empty garbage under PCG
    replay, so NaN/Inf cannot reach residual / MoE routing / allreduce."""
    pcg_static_tokens = context.num_tokens
    actual_tokens = context.raw_num_tokens
    if (
        pcg_static_tokens is not None
        and actual_tokens is not None
        and pcg_static_tokens > actual_tokens
    ):
        first_dim = buf.shape[0]
        elems_per_token = buf.numel() // first_dim
        buf.view(first_dim, elems_per_token)[actual_tokens:].zero_()


if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.layers.quantization.base_config import QuantizationConfig
    from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_batch_info import ForwardBatch


class AttentionType(Enum):
    """
    Attention type.
    Use string to be compatible with `torch.compile`.
    """

    # Decoder attention between previous layer Q/K/V
    DECODER = "decoder"
    # Decoder bidirectional attention between image tokens
    DECODER_BIDIRECTIONAL = "decoder_bidirectional"
    # Encoder attention between previous layer Q/K/V
    ENCODER_ONLY = "encoder_only"


class RadixAttention(nn.Module):
    """
    The attention layer implementation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        num_heads: int,
        head_dim: int,
        scaling: float,
        num_kv_heads: int,
        layer_id: int,
        logit_cap: float = 0.0,
        v_head_dim: int = -1,
        sliding_window_size: int = -1,
        is_cross_attention: bool = False,
        pos_encoding_mode: str = "NONE",
        logit_capping_method: str = "tanh",
        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
        attn_type: AttentionType = AttentionType.DECODER,
        use_irope: bool = False,
        prefix: str = "",
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.tp_q_head_num = num_heads
        self.tp_k_head_num = num_kv_heads
        self.tp_v_head_num = num_kv_heads
        self.head_dim = head_dim
        self.qk_head_dim = head_dim
        self.v_head_dim = v_head_dim if v_head_dim != -1 else head_dim
        self.scaling = scaling
        self.layer_id = layer_id
        self.logit_cap = logit_cap
        self.sliding_window_size = sliding_window_size or -1
        self.is_cross_attention = is_cross_attention
        self.use_irope = use_irope
        self.k_scale = None
        self.v_scale = None
        self.k_scale_float = None
        self.v_scale_float = None
        self.quant_method = None

        if quant_config is not None:
            self.quant_method = quant_config.get_quant_method(self, prefix=prefix)
        if self.quant_method is not None:
            self.quant_method.create_weights(self)
        self.attn_type = attn_type

        self.pos_encoding_mode = pos_encoding_mode
        self.logit_capping_method = logit_capping_method
        self.xai_temperature_len = -1

    def forward(
        self,
        q,
        k,
        v,
        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
        save_kv_cache: bool = True,
        key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        if k is not None:
            # For cross-layer sharing, kv can be None
            assert v is not None
            if "k_rope" not in kwargs:
                k = k.view(-1, self.tp_k_head_num, self.qk_head_dim)
                v = v.view(-1, self.tp_v_head_num, self.v_head_dim)
            else:
                k = k.view(-1, self.tp_k_head_num, self.v_head_dim)

        context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
        if (
            forward_batch.forward_mode.is_extend()
            and context is not None
            # ``_force_eager_attn`` is only set inside Inkling's eager
            # norm+attn+sconv region, never during tc-piecewise capture. Reading
            # the ContextVar under the fullgraph torch.compile trace is
            # untraceable ("Unsupported method call: ContextVar.get"), so
            # short-circuit it while compiling -- force-eager is always off there.
            and (torch.compiler.is_compiling() or not _force_eager_attn.get())
        ):
            if kwargs.get("idx_q") is not None:
                if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
                    return get_attn_backend().forward(
                        q, k, v, self, forward_batch, save_kv_cache, **kwargs
                    )
                idx_q = kwargs["idx_q"]
                idx_k = kwargs["idx_k"]
                idx_v = kwargs.get("idx_v")
                attn_out = q.new_empty(
                    (q.shape[0], self.tp_q_head_num * self.v_head_dim)
                )
                idx_out = q.new_empty((q.shape[0], idx_q.shape[1] * idx_q.shape[2]))
                unified_sparse_attention_with_output(
                    q,
                    k,
                    v,
                    attn_out,
                    idx_out,
                    idx_q,
                    idx_k,
                    save_kv_cache,
                    self.layer_id,
                    idx_v=idx_v,
                )
                return idx_out, attn_out
            # FP8 q (e.g. mxfp8 KV-cache attention) still produces a bf16
            # attention output; sizing the buffer off q's dtype would silently
            # cast-copy the result to fp8.
            out_dtype = (
                torch.bfloat16
                if q.dtype in (torch.float8_e4m3fn, torch.float8_e5m2)
                else q.dtype
            )
            if self.qk_head_dim != self.v_head_dim:
                output = q.new_empty(
                    (q.shape[0], self.tp_q_head_num * self.v_head_dim),
                    dtype=out_dtype,
                )
            else:
                output = torch.empty_like(q, dtype=out_dtype)
            if any(
                key in kwargs
                for key in (
                    "score_mod",
                    "aux_tensors",
                    "rel_bias",
                    "q_descale",
                    "k_descale",
                    "v_descale",
                )
            ):
                # A score_mod callable, aux_tensors, rel_bias, or mxfp8 descale
                # tensors can't cross the unified_attention_with_output custom-op
                # schema; route this backend's extend attention through the plain
                # eager path.
                if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
                    breakable_attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
                        q, k, v, output, save_kv_cache, self.layer_id, kwargs
                    )
                else:
                    attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
                        q, k, v, output, save_kv_cache, self.layer_id, kwargs
                    )
                return output
            # Chunked-prefix MHA needs LSE to merge independently normalized
            # suffix and cached-prefix attention states.
            return_lse = bool(forward_batch.mha_return_lse)
            mha_companion_layers = context.mha_companion_layers
            use_mha_companion = (
                mha_companion_layers is not None
                and mha_companion_layers[self.layer_id] is self
            )
            if is_in_breakable_cuda_graph():
                op = (
                    breakable_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
                    if return_lse
                    else breakable_unified_attention_with_output
                )
            else:
                op = (
                    unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
                    if return_lse
                    else unified_attention_with_output
                )
            lse = op(
                q,
                k,
                v,
                output,
                save_kv_cache,
                self.layer_id,
                use_mha_companion=use_mha_companion,
                key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
                **kwargs,
            )
            if return_lse:
                return output.view(-1, self.tp_q_head_num, self.v_head_dim), lse
            return output
        else:
            return get_attn_backend().forward(
                q,
                k,
                v,
                self,
                forward_batch,
                save_kv_cache,
                **kwargs,
            )


def _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    output: torch.Tensor,
    save_kv_cache: bool,
    layer_id: int,
    use_mha_companion: bool,
    return_lse: bool,
    *,
    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    # MLA / TRT-LLM / NSA paths pass these through RadixAttention.forward(**kwargs);
    # they must appear in the schema when --enforce-piecewise-cuda-graph is on.
    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
    attention_layers = context.attention_layers
    attention_layer = attention_layers[layer_id]
    real_query_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu
    # Ordinary PCG attention pads Q/K/V to the same token bucket. Prefix MHA
    # instead supplies a fixed-capacity K/V chunk whose extent is independent
    # of the suffix queries, so its caller must preserve that separate extent.
    if key_value_num_tokens is None:
        key_value_num_tokens = real_query_num_tokens

    query = query[:real_query_num_tokens]
    if key is not None:
        key = key[:key_value_num_tokens]
    if value is not None:
        value = value[:key_value_num_tokens]

    # DeepSeek MLA has two RadixAttention instances per layer (attn_mqa and
    # attn_mha) that share the same layer_id. Preserve the calling instance's
    # identity through the custom-op boundary; save_kv_cache is not an identity
    # signal because absorbed MLA can also disable a redundant cache store.
    if use_mha_companion:
        assert context.mha_companion_layers is not None
        attention_layer = context.mha_companion_layers[layer_id]
        assert attention_layer is not None

    kwargs = {}
    if q_rope is not None:
        kwargs["q_rope"] = q_rope[:real_query_num_tokens]
    if k_rope is not None:
        kwargs["k_rope"] = k_rope[:key_value_num_tokens]
    if sinks is not None:
        kwargs["sinks"] = sinks
    if cos_sin_cache is not None:
        kwargs["cos_sin_cache"] = cos_sin_cache
    if is_neox is not None:
        kwargs["is_neox"] = is_neox
    if llama_4_scaling is not None:
        kwargs["llama_4_scaling"] = llama_4_scaling
    if topk_indices is not None:
        kwargs["topk_indices"] = topk_indices[:real_query_num_tokens]

    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
    # Keep the original ForwardBatch object and only narrow cache locations for
    # this backend call so model/backend state is still written to the same batch.
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_query_num_tokens]

    # Store pre-allocated output for FA backend to write directly into.
    # Must slice to real_query_num_tokens to match the narrowed query shape —
    # the FA kernel validates out.size(0) == q.size(0).
    forward_batch._attn_output = output[:real_query_num_tokens]

    ret = get_attn_backend().forward(
        query,
        key,
        value,
        attention_layer,
        forward_batch,
        save_kv_cache,
        **kwargs,
    )
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc

    lse = None
    if return_lse:
        assert isinstance(ret, tuple)
        ret, lse, *_ = ret
    else:
        assert isinstance(ret, torch.Tensor)

    if ret.data_ptr() != output.data_ptr():
        output[:real_query_num_tokens].view(ret.shape).copy_(ret)

    # During PCG replay the attention backend writes only the narrowed
    # real-token slice (output[:real_query_num_tokens]) and leaves padded positions
    # as uninitialized torch.empty garbage. Zero them so garbage (NaN/Inf) does
    # not propagate through residual connections, MoE routing, and allreduce.
    # This affects every backend that varlen-writes under PCG, not just ROCm.
    # Use context.raw_num_tokens (pre-padding count from PCG runner) instead of
    # forward_batch.extend_num_tokens, which is None for TARGET_VERIFY batches.
    _zero_padded_pcg_tail(output, context)
    if lse is not None and lse.shape[0] != output.shape[0]:
        padded_lse = lse.new_zeros((output.shape[0], *lse.shape[1:]))
        padded_lse[:real_query_num_tokens].copy_(lse)
        lse = padded_lse
    return lse


@register_custom_op(mutates_args=["output"])
@register_split_op()
def unified_attention_with_output(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    output: torch.Tensor,
    save_kv_cache: bool,
    layer_id: int,
    *,
    use_mha_companion: bool = False,
    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> None:
    _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
        query,
        key,
        value,
        output,
        save_kv_cache,
        layer_id,
        use_mha_companion,
        False,
        key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
        q_rope=q_rope,
        k_rope=k_rope,
        sinks=sinks,
        cos_sin_cache=cos_sin_cache,
        is_neox=is_neox,
        llama_4_scaling=llama_4_scaling,
        topk_indices=topk_indices,
    )


def _unified_attention_with_output_and_lse_fake(
    query: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs
) -> torch.Tensor:
    return query.new_empty((query.shape[0], query.shape[1]), dtype=torch.float32)


@register_custom_op(
    mutates_args=["output"], fake_impl=_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse_fake
)
@register_split_op()
def unified_attention_with_output_and_lse(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    output: torch.Tensor,
    save_kv_cache: bool,
    layer_id: int,
    *,
    use_mha_companion: bool = False,
    key_value_num_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    q_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    k_rope: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    cos_sin_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    is_neox: Optional[bool] = None,
    llama_4_scaling: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    topk_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    lse = _unified_attention_with_output_impl(
        query,
        key,
        value,
        output,
        save_kv_cache,
        layer_id,
        use_mha_companion,
        True,
        key_value_num_tokens=key_value_num_tokens,
        q_rope=q_rope,
        k_rope=k_rope,
        sinks=sinks,
        cos_sin_cache=cos_sin_cache,
        is_neox=is_neox,
        llama_4_scaling=llama_4_scaling,
        topk_indices=topk_indices,
    )
    assert lse is not None
    return lse


@register_custom_op(mutates_args=["attn_out", "idx_out"])
@register_split_op()
def unified_sparse_attention_with_output(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    attn_out: torch.Tensor,
    idx_out: torch.Tensor,
    idx_q: torch.Tensor,
    idx_k: torch.Tensor,
    save_kv_cache: bool,
    layer_id: int,
    *,
    idx_v: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> None:
    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
    attention_layer = context.attention_layers[layer_id]
    real_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu

    query = query[:real_num_tokens]
    if key is not None:
        key = key[:real_num_tokens]
    if value is not None:
        value = value[:real_num_tokens]
    idx_q = idx_q[:real_num_tokens]
    idx_k = idx_k[:real_num_tokens]
    if idx_v is not None:
        idx_v = idx_v[:real_num_tokens]

    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_num_tokens]

    ret_idx, ret_out = get_attn_backend().forward(
        query,
        key,
        value,
        attention_layer,
        forward_batch,
        save_kv_cache,
        idx_q=idx_q,
        idx_k=idx_k,
        idx_v=idx_v,
    )
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc

    attn_out[:real_num_tokens].view(ret_out.shape).copy_(ret_out)
    # disable_value layers return ret_idx=None; the guard keeps idx_out's
    # untouched real-token slice safe (model returns before index_o_proj).
    if ret_idx is not None:
        idx_out[:real_num_tokens].view(ret_idx.shape).copy_(ret_idx)

    for buf in (attn_out, idx_out):
        _zero_padded_pcg_tail(buf, context)
    return


breakable_unified_attention_with_output = eager_on_graph(True)(
    unified_attention_with_output
)
breakable_unified_attention_with_output_and_lse = eager_on_graph(True)(
    unified_attention_with_output_and_lse
)


def attention_with_output_extra_kwargs(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    value: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    output: torch.Tensor,
    save_kv_cache: bool,
    layer_id: int,
    extra_kwargs: dict,
) -> None:
    """Breakable/tc_piecewise attention for backends whose forward needs kwargs
    that cannot cross the ``unified_attention_with_output`` custom-op schema --
    a ``score_mod`` callable and/or ``aux_tensors`` (e.g. Inkling's relative-bias
    fa4 attention). Plain (not a custom op) so the callable passes through; still
    runs eagerly between graph segments under BCG via the wrapper below. Mirrors
    the real-token narrowing + padded-output write of
    ``unified_attention_with_output``, and narrows per-token ``aux_tensors`` too.
    """
    context = get_tc_piecewise_forward_context()
    forward_batch = context.forward_batch
    attention_layer = context.attention_layers[layer_id]
    real_num_tokens = forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu

    query = query[:real_num_tokens]
    if key is not None:
        key = key[:real_num_tokens]
    if value is not None:
        value = value[:real_num_tokens]

    kwargs = dict(extra_kwargs)
    aux_tensors = kwargs.get("aux_tensors")
    if aux_tensors is not None:
        kwargs["aux_tensors"] = [t[:real_num_tokens] for t in aux_tensors]
    for per_token_key in ("rel_bias", "q_descale", "k_descale", "v_descale"):
        t = kwargs.get(per_token_key)
        if t is not None:
            kwargs[per_token_key] = t[:real_num_tokens]

    original_out_cache_loc = forward_batch.out_cache_loc
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc[:real_num_tokens]
    forward_batch._attn_output = output[:real_num_tokens]

    ret = get_attn_backend().forward(
        query, key, value, attention_layer, forward_batch, save_kv_cache, **kwargs
    )
    forward_batch.out_cache_loc = original_out_cache_loc

    if ret.data_ptr() != output.data_ptr():
        output[:real_num_tokens].view(ret.shape).copy_(ret)

    if _is_hip:
        _zero_padded_pcg_tail(output, context)
    return


breakable_attention_with_output_extra_kwargs = eager_on_graph(True)(
    attention_with_output_extra_kwargs
)

file.content:
"""Per-forward-call control context.

Owns ForwardContext — a frozen dataclass holding control configs the model
layer reads at depth via get_forward_context(). The only mandatory field
today is attn_backend; pool refs are derived from attn_backend.*
(every backend caches them at __init__), so a published ForwardContext
is enough to resolve the active pools without a separate global.

ModelRunner._forward_raw publishes a fresh ForwardContext for the
duration of each forward; callers that need a per-call override (PDmux
per-stream backend, frozen-KV MTP draft loop, TBO per-child dispatch) use
dataclasses.replace and wrap the override scope with forward_context().

Distinct from sglang.srt.model_executor.runner_backend_utils.tc_piecewise_cuda_graph.TcPiecewiseForwardContext,
which collects compilation-time refs for the piecewise CUDA graph backend.

Concurrency: _current is a plain module-level global, not thread-local.
This matches the global_server_args precedent and is safe because each
forward runs synchronously on a single Python thread per worker process. If
worker threads ever share a process, migrate to contextvars.ContextVar.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from contextlib import contextmanager
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Optional

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.base_attn_backend import AttentionBackend
    from sglang.srt.mem_cache.memory_pool import KVCache, ReqToTokenPool


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class ForwardContext:
    """Per-forward-call control configs. Read via get_forward_context();
    extend by adding fields here. Frozen so accidental mutation raises at
    write time — use dataclasses.replace for per-call overrides."""

    attn_backend: AttentionBackend


_current: Optional[ForwardContext] = None


def set_forward_context(ctx: Optional[ForwardContext]) -> Optional[ForwardContext]:
    """Set the active context; return the previous one for explicit
    save/restore. Prefer the forward_context() context manager."""
    global _current
    prev, _current = _current, ctx
    return prev


def has_forward_context() -> bool:
    return _current is not None


def get_forward_context() -> ForwardContext:
    assert _current is not None, (
        "no forward context active — call forward_context(...) or set_forward_context(...) "
        "before reading get_forward_context()."
    )
    return _current


def get_attn_backend() -> AttentionBackend:
    return get_forward_context().attn_backend


def get_token_to_kv_pool() -> KVCache:
    return get_attn_backend().token_to_kv_pool


def get_req_to_token_pool() -> ReqToTokenPool:
    return get_attn_backend().req_to_token_pool


@contextmanager
def forward_context(ctx: ForwardContext):
    prev = set_forward_context(ctx)

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List, Optional

import torch
import triton

from sglang.kernels.ops.attention.metadata import get_num_kv_splits_triton
from sglang.kernels.ops.kvcache.kv_indices import (
    create_flashinfer_kv_indices_triton,
)
from sglang.srt.configs.hybrid_arch import (
    hybrid_gdn_config,
    kimi_linear_config,
    linear_attn_model_spec,
)
from sglang.srt.configs.model_config import AttentionArch
from sglang.srt.distributed.device_communicators.pynccl_allocator import (
    use_symmetric_memory,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs
from sglang.srt.layers.attention.base_attn_backend import AttentionBackend
from sglang.srt.layers.dcp import (
    cp_lse_ag_out_rs_mha,
    create_triton_kv_indices_for_dcp_triton,
    get_dcp_lens,
)
from sglang.srt.layers.radix_attention import AttentionType
from sglang.srt.mem_cache.memory_pool import KVWriteLoc
from sglang.srt.mem_cache.swa_memory_pool import SWAKVPool
from sglang.srt.model_executor.cuda_graph_config import cuda_graph_fully_disabled
from sglang.srt.model_executor.forward_batch_info import ForwardBatch, ForwardMode
from sglang.srt.runtime_context import get_parallel
from sglang.srt.speculative.spec_utils import (
    draft_kv_indices_buffer_width,
    draft_kv_indices_used_len,
    generate_draft_decode_kv_indices,
)
from sglang.srt.utils import (
    get_bool_env_var,
    get_device_core_count,
    get_int_env_var,
    is_cuda,
    is_gfx95_supported,
    is_gfx942_supported,
    is_xpu,
    next_power_of_2,
)

_is_cuda = is_cuda()
_is_gfx942 = is_gfx942_supported()
_is_xpu = is_xpu()

if _is_cuda:
    from sgl_kernel.utils import is_arch_support_pdl

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.layers.radix_attention import RadixAttention
    from sglang.srt.model_executor.model_runner import ModelRunner
    from sglang.srt.speculative.spec_info import SpecInput


_MLA_DECODE_MIN_BLOCK_KV = 32


def _mla_decode_kv_splits_cap(
    base_max_kv_splits: int, sm_count: int, max_context_len: int
) -> int:
    if sm_count <= 0:
        return base_max_kv_splits
    sm_cap = next_power_of_2(sm_count)
    ctx_cap = next_power_of_2(triton.cdiv(max_context_len, _MLA_DECODE_MIN_BLOCK_KV))
    return max(base_max_kv_splits, min(sm_cap, ctx_cap))


def logit_capping_mod(logit_capping_method, logit_cap):
    # positive logit_cap -> tanh cap
    if logit_capping_method == "tanh":
        return logit_cap
    else:
        raise ValueError()


@dataclass
class ForwardMetadata:
    attn_logits: torch.Tensor
    attn_lse: torch.Tensor
    max_extend_len: int
    num_kv_splits: torch.Tensor
    kv_indptr: torch.Tensor
    kv_indices: torch.Tensor
    qo_indptr: torch.Tensor
    custom_mask: torch.Tensor
    mask_indptr: torch.Tensor
    # Sliding window
    window_kv_indptr: torch.Tensor
    window_kv_indices: torch.Tensor
    window_num_kv_splits: torch.Tensor
    window_kv_offsets: torch.Tensor
    # Separate attn_logits for SWA layers when v_head_dim differs
    swa_attn_logits: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    # full->SWA translated out_cache_loc (SWA KV-store write target)
    swa_out_cache_loc: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    # PHYSICAL full-attn write target for the unified pool (eager: translated tensor;
    # cuda-graph: capture-stable buffer view). None for non-unified pools.
    out_cache_loc_full_physical: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None


class TritonAttnBackend(AttentionBackend):
    # CUDA-graph replay rebuilds metadata from preallocated kv_indptr/kv_indices
    # buffers; it never reads seq_lens_cpu / seq_lens_sum.
    needs_cpu_seq_lens: bool = False

    def __init__(
        self,
        model_runner: ModelRunner,
        skip_prefill: bool = False,
        kv_indptr_buf: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    ):
        # Lazy import to avoid the initialization of cuda context
        from sglang.kernels.ops.attention.decode_attention import (
            decode_attention_fwd,
        )
        from sglang.kernels.ops.attention.extend_attention import (
            build_unified_kv_indices,
            extend_attention_fwd,
            extend_attention_fwd_unified,
        )
        from sglang.kernels.ops.attention.verify_splitkv import (

25	class ResolvedAttentionBackendStr(msgspec.Struct, frozen=True, kw_only=True):
26	    prefill: str
27	    decode: str
28	    is_draft_override: bool = False
29	
30	
31	class AttentionBackends(msgspec.Struct, frozen=True, kw_only=True):
32	    attn_backend: AttentionBackend
33	    decode_attn_backend: Optional[AttentionBackend]
34	    decode_attn_backend_group: list[AttentionBackend]
35	    prefill_attention_backend_str: str
36	    decode_attention_backend_str: str
37	
38	
39	def configure_aux_hidden_state_capture(
40	    *,
41	    model,
42	    eagle_use_aux_hidden_state: bool,
43	    eagle_aux_hidden_state_layer_ids,
44	    dflash_use_aux_hidden_state: bool,
45	    dflash_target_layer_ids,
46	    is_dspark: bool,
47	) -> None:
48	    """Configure auxiliary hidden state capture for speculative decoding.
49	
50	    Must be called before CUDA graph capture so the captured graphs
51	    include aux hidden state output paths.
52	    """
53	    if eagle_use_aux_hidden_state:
54	        model.set_eagle3_layers_to_capture(eagle_aux_hidden_state_layer_ids)
55	    if dflash_use_aux_hidden_state:
56	        if is_dspark and hasattr(model, "set_dspark_layers_to_capture"):
57	            model.set_dspark_layers_to_capture(dflash_target_layer_ids)
58	        elif hasattr(model, "set_dflash_layers_to_capture"):
59	            model.set_dflash_layers_to_capture(dflash_target_layer_ids)
60	        else:
61	            raise ValueError(
62	                f"Model {model.__class__.__name__} implements neither "
63	                "set_dspark_layers_to_capture nor set_dflash_layers_to_capture, "
64	                "one of which is required for DFLASH/DSPARK."
65	            )
66	
67	
68	def build_attention_backends(*, model_runner: ModelRunner) -> AttentionBackends:
69	    """Init attention kernel backend."""
70	    server_args = model_runner.server_args
71	
72	    # TODO: Refactor device-specific init branches into platform interface (separate PR).
73	    if model_runner.device in ("cuda", "musa"):
74	        init_cublas()
75	
76	    resolved = _resolve_attention_backend_strs(
77	        server_args=server_args, is_draft_worker=model_runner.is_draft_worker
78	    )
79	
80	    if server_args.enable_pdmux:
81	        attn_backend = _build_resolved_backend(
82	            model_runner=model_runner, resolved=resolved, init_new_workspace=True
83	        )
84	        decode_attn_backend_group = [
85	            _build_resolved_backend(
86	                model_runner=model_runner,
87	                resolved=resolved,
88	                init_new_workspace=False,
89	            )
90	            for _ in range(server_args.sm_group_num)
91	        ]
92	        decode_attn_backend = decode_attn_backend_group[0]
93	    elif server_args.enable_two_batch_overlap and not model_runner.is_draft_worker:
94	        attn_backend = TboAttnBackend.init_new(
95	            lambda: _build_resolved_backend(
96	                model_runner=model_runner,
97	                resolved=resolved,
98	                init_new_workspace=False,
99	            )
100	        )
101	        decode_attn_backend = None
102	        decode_attn_backend_group = []
103	    else:
104	        attn_backend = _build_resolved_backend(
105	            model_runner=model_runner, resolved=resolved, init_new_workspace=False
106	        )
107	        decode_attn_backend = None
108	        decode_attn_backend_group = []
109	
110	    if (
111	        model_runner.device == "npu"
112	        and envs.SGLANG_ZBAL_LOCAL_MEM_SIZE.get() > 0
113	        and not model_runner.is_draft_worker
114	    ):
115	        # lazy init for zbal with mix mode (before graph capture when enable_cuda_graph)
116	        from sglang.srt.hardware_backend.npu.utils import lazy_init_zbal_gva_mem
117	
118	        lazy_init_zbal_gva_mem(
119	            model_runner.device,
120	            model_runner.gpu_id,
121	            get_world_group().rank_in_group,
122	            get_world_group().world_size,
123	            get_world_group().cpu_group,
124	        )
125	
126	    # Record resolved per-mode backends on the backend for model dispatch.
127	    attn_backend.prefill_attention_backend_str = resolved.prefill
128	    attn_backend.decode_attention_backend_str = resolved.decode
129	
130	    return AttentionBackends(
131	        attn_backend=attn_backend,
132	        decode_attn_backend=decode_attn_backend,
133	        decode_attn_backend_group=decode_attn_backend_group,
134	        prefill_attention_backend_str=resolved.prefill,
135	        decode_attention_backend_str=resolved.decode,
136	    )
137	
138	
139	def get_attention_backend(
140	    *, model_runner: ModelRunner, init_new_workspace: bool = False
141	) -> AttentionBackend:
142	    """Init attention kernel backend."""
143	    resolved = _resolve_attention_backend_strs(
144	        server_args=model_runner.server_args,
145	        is_draft_worker=model_runner.is_draft_worker,
146	    )
147	    return _build_resolved_backend(
148	        model_runner=model_runner,
149	        resolved=resolved,
150	        init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
151	    )
152	
153	
154	def _resolve_attention_backend_strs(
155	    *, server_args: ServerArgs, is_draft_worker: bool
156	) -> ResolvedAttentionBackendStr:
157	    draft_attn_backend = server_args.speculative_draft_attention_backend
158	    if is_draft_worker and draft_attn_backend:
159	        logger.warning(f"Overriding draft attention backend to {draft_attn_backend}.")
160	        # Single backend for all draft modes (no prefill/decode split).
161	        return ResolvedAttentionBackendStr(
162	            prefill=draft_attn_backend,
163	            decode=draft_attn_backend,
164	            is_draft_override=True,
165	        )
166	    prefill, decode = server_args.get_attention_backends()
167	    return ResolvedAttentionBackendStr(prefill=prefill, decode=decode)
168	
169	
170	def _build_resolved_backend(
171	    *,
172	    model_runner: ModelRunner,
173	    resolved: ResolvedAttentionBackendStr,
174	    init_new_workspace: bool,
175	) -> AttentionBackend:
176	    if resolved.is_draft_override:
177	        attn_backend = _build_backend_from_str(
178	            model_runner=model_runner,
179	            backend_str=resolved.prefill,
180	            init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
181	        )
182	    elif resolved.decode != resolved.prefill:
183	        from sglang.srt.layers.attention.hybrid_attn_backend import (
184	            HybridAttnBackend,
185	        )
186	
187	        # Compose the two full-attention backends first, then apply model-level
188	        # wrappers once.  Wrapping each child independently duplicates the
189	        # linear/sparse side backend for hybrid models (for example, two GDN
190	        # dispatchers for Qwen3.5 when prefill and decode use different MHA
191	        # backends), duplicating initialization and associated state while only
192	        # one side backend can be active in a forward pass.
193	        attn_backend = attn_backend_wrapper(
194	            model_runner,
195	            HybridAttnBackend(
196	                model_runner=model_runner,
197	                decode_backend=_build_full_attention_backend_from_str(
198	                    model_runner=model_runner,
199	                    backend_str=resolved.decode,
200	                    init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
201	                ),
202	                prefill_backend=_build_full_attention_backend_from_str(
203	                    model_runner=model_runner,
204	                    backend_str=resolved.prefill,
205	                    init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
206	                ),
207	            ),
208	        )
209	        logger.info(
210	            f"Using hybrid attention backend for decode and prefill: "
211	            f"decode_backend={resolved.decode}, "
212	            f"prefill_backend={resolved.prefill}."
213	        )
214	        logger.warning(
215	            "Warning: Attention backend specified by --attention-backend or default backend might be overridden."
216	            "The feature of hybrid attention backend is experimental and unstable. Please raise an issue if you encounter any problem."
217	        )
218	    else:
219	        attn_backend = _build_backend_from_str(
220	            model_runner=model_runner,
221	            backend_str=model_runner.server_args.attention_backend,
222	            init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
223	        )
224	    return attn_backend
225	
226	
227	def _build_backend_from_str(
228	    *, model_runner: ModelRunner, backend_str: str, init_new_workspace: bool
229	) -> AttentionBackend:
230	    return attn_backend_wrapper(
231	        model_runner,
232	        _build_full_attention_backend_from_str(
233	            model_runner=model_runner,
234	            backend_str=backend_str,
235	            init_new_workspace=init_new_workspace,
236	        ),
237	    )
238	
239	
240	def _build_full_attention_backend_from_str(
241	    *, model_runner: ModelRunner, backend_str: str, init_new_workspace: bool
242	) -> AttentionBackend:
243	    if backend_str not in ATTENTION_BACKENDS:
244	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid attention backend: {backend_str}")
245	    model_runner.init_new_workspace = init_new_workspace
246	    return ATTENTION_BACKENDS[backend_str](model_runner)
247

file.content:
class FlashInferAttnBackend(AttentionBackend):
    """Flashinfer attention kernels."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        model_runner: ModelRunner,
        skip_prefill: bool = False,
        kv_indptr_buf: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
        kv_last_page_len_buf: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
        init_new_workspace: bool = False,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.prefill_backend = "fa2"
        self.decode_backend = "fa2"

        self.req_to_token_pool = model_runner.req_to_token_pool
        self.token_to_kv_pool = model_runner.token_to_kv_pool
        self._swa_kv_pool: Optional[BaseSWAKVPool] = self._resolve_swa_kv_pool(
            model_runner
        )
        self.use_sliding_window_kv_pool = self._swa_kv_pool is not None
        self.enable_mis = model_runner.server_args.enable_mis

        # FIXME: remove dllm workarounds from flashinfer
        self.dllm_config = DllmConfig.from_server_args(model_runner.server_args)
        self.is_dllm_model = self.dllm_config is not None

        self.kv_cache_quant_method = self.token_to_kv_pool.get_kv_cache_quant_method()
        self.prefill_kv_access = self.kv_cache_quant_method.resolve_attention_access(
            "prefill", "flashinfer"
        )
        self.decode_kv_access = self.kv_cache_quant_method.resolve_attention_access(
            "decode", "flashinfer"
        )
        prefill_backend, decode_backend = (
            model_runner.server_args.get_attention_backends()
        )
        if self.__class__ is FlashInferAttnBackend:
            if prefill_backend == "flashinfer":
                self._check_kv_attention_access("prefill", self.prefill_kv_access)

Let me note the key insights for each section:

4.1 概念: Why pluggable attention backends?
- Attention is the hottest op in LLM inference; different hardware (NVIDIA Hopper/Blackwell, AMD ROCm, Intel, NPU Ascend, MUSA) and different model architectures (standard MHA, GQA, MLA, hybrid linear/mamba, sparse) need different kernels.
- A single attention implementation can't be optimal everywhere. SGLang separates the "attention as a layer" (RadixAttention, part of the model graph) from the "attention kernel implementation" (backend).
- RadixAttention.forward just calls get_attn_backend().forward(...) — it doesn't know which kernel runs. The backend is chosen once at startup.
- Backends read the same paged KV pool (TokenToKVPool) via out_cache_loc, so the cache layer is decoupled from compute.

4.2 注册与选择机制 (Registry & selection):
- attention_registry.py holds ATTENTION_BACKENDS dict + register_attention_backend decorator.
- Each backend registered by a factory function create_xxx_backend(runner) → returns an AttentionBackend instance.
- ATTENTION_BACKEND_CHOICES in server_args.py is the CLI argparse choices list (hpc_ops added at L214).
- Selection chain: ModelRunner.init_attention_backends → build_attention_backends (attention_backend_setup.py) → _resolve_attention_backend_strs → get_attention_backends (prefill/decode split) → _build_full_attention_backend_from_str → ATTENTION_BACKENDS[backend_str](runner).
- Default backend auto-detected in _attention_backend_default (server_args.py ~L5310): flashinfer on most CUDA, trtllm_mha on Blackwell sm100, fa3 on Hopper+cuda12.3, aiter on HIP, triton fallback.
- prefill/decode can use different backends → HybridAttnBackend composes them.
- hpc_ops must be explicitly set via --attention-backend hpc_ops (it's never auto-selected).

4.3 AttentionBackend 抽象基类契约 (base class):
- forward() dispatches by forward_mode: idle → empty, decode → forward_decode, extend → forward_extend, mixed (npu) → forward_mixed. Subclasses implement forward_extend/forward_decode.
- init_forward_metadata 3-method contract: init_forward_metadata (eager entry, default = out_graph + in_graph), init_forward_metadata_out_graph (host/dynamic, outside graph capture), init_forward_metadata_in_graph (graph-recordable static-shape GPU op, no .item()/.cpu()).
- init_cuda_graph_state: allocate static buffers for decode CUDA graph replay.
- needs_cpu_seq_lens: whether backend needs seq_lens on CPU (D2H sync). hpc_ops/triton/trtllm_mha set False (build page table on-device).
- get_cuda_graph_seq_len_fill_value: 0 or 1 (padding value).

4.4 FlashInfer 与 Triton:
- FlashInferAttnBackend: wraps FlashInfer library (fa2 prefill/decode kernels). Most general CUDA backend, supports sliding window, custom mask, MLA via FlashInferMLAAttnBackend. sets self.prefill_backend="fa2".
- TritonAttnBackend: pure Triton kernels (extend_attention_fwd, decode_attention_fwd), no external lib dependency, builds kv_indices on device, needs_cpu_seq_lens=False. Used as fallback, also for MLA decode on AMD.
- Both require a non-encoder-decoder model for some features (triton asserts no cross attention).

4.5 HPC-Ops 后端:
- Powered by Tencent HPC-Ops library (https://github.com/Tencent/hpc-ops). Wraps paged MHA kernels attention_with_kvcache_prefill_bf16 (extend) and attention_decode_bf16 (decode).
- Reads SGLang token-major KV pool as paged NHD cache (num_pages, page_size, num_kv_heads, head_dim) with NO copy.
- Hard constraints (from docstring + create_hpc_ops_backend + __init__):
  - Hopper+ (sm90+)
  - --page-size 64 (also enforced in overrides.py, auto-corrects)
  - bf16 model dtype; bf16 or fp8_e4m3 KV cache
  - non-MLA (create_hpc_ops_backend raises if use_mla_backend)
  - non-encoder-decoder (create_hpc_ops_backend raises; also triton)
  - no speculative decoding (create_hpc_ops_backend raises if speculative_algorithm is not None; also __init__ checks spec_algorithm.is_speculative())
  - head_dim == 128 and num_q_heads // num_kv_heads in {4, 8} (GQA group size)
  - no sliding window, no logit cap, default softmax scaling (head_dim ** -0.5)
  - FP8 path requires model to run fused_qk_rope_store_kv_fp8 (wired for HunYuan V3), and per-rank (num_q_heads, num_kv_heads) in {(8,1),(64,8)}
- needs_cpu_seq_lens = False (page table built on-device, reuse trtllm_mha helpers build_trtllm_mha_page_table / update_trtllm_mha_graph_metadata).
- Tuned for H20; on H100/H200/B200 speedup may be limited.

Now for the practices — since this is hard to actually run (needs Hopper GPU + hpc package), I'll provide both:
- A "switch backend" practice (run with flashinfer/triton — runnable on any CUDA)
- A "source-reading" practice for hpc_ops constraints (locate the validation code)
